scenes.voiceover: If you can't save a lot, it's not worth saving at all.
If you can't pay off all your debt, why pay extra?
This black-and-white thinking prevents progress.
You're waiting for the day when you have enough to make a "real" difference.
That day never comes.
In reality, small steps add up.
Saving $20 a week is over $1,000 a year.
An emergency fund for a car repair.
Paying an extra $50 monthly on credit cards saves hundreds in interest.
Contributing even 3% to retirement grows over time.
In the US, people are overwhelmed by big financial goals.
$30,000 in student loans.
$10,000 emergency fund.
A million for retirement.
These feel impossible, so people don't try.
But small, consistent actions create big results.
The problem is your brain doesn't see it that way.
Small actions don't feel rewarding.
Breaking this means celebrating small wins.
Every $20 saved is progress.
Stop waiting for perfect conditions.
